skill.name: reporting-issues
skill.description: Use this skill when you need to report on a troubleshooting session
skill.summary: Use this skill when you need to report on a troubleshooting session
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: The static high finding for weak cryptography is a false positive. SKILL.md line 3 contains only a plain troubleshooting report description, and no cryptographic algorithm, executable code, network access, or prompt injection text was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3 for weak cryptography, but the line only describes reporting on a troubleshooting session. No hash algorithm, encryption routine, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is natural-language metadata, not code. The full skill file has only frontmatter and one instruction delegating report creation.

content.value_statement: Troubleshooting work can lose important context when notes stay informal. This skill routes the session to a report generator so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can produce a clear issue report.
actual_capabilities: Recognizes requests to report on a troubleshooting session.
Delegates report creation to the named troubleshoot-report-generator agent.
Helps turn troubleshooting context into a structured written report.
Supports issue reporting workflows in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
Provides a focused handoff point after diagnostic work is complete.
limitations: It does not include its own report template in the skill file.
It depends on the troubleshoot-report-generator agent being available.
It does not collect logs, run diagnostics, or verify technical facts by itself.
It cannot guarantee report completeness without enough session context.

use_cases.title: Support Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a concise report after a support investigation so another team can continue the work.

use_cases.title: Bug Escalation
use_cases.description: Summarize symptoms, attempted fixes, and current status before opening an engineering issue.

use_cases.title: Incident Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Document a troubleshooting session for records, review, or a post-incident action list.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Session Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a short troubleshooting summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a troubleshooting report from this session. Include the problem, actions taken, current result, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Support Ticket Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a customer or internal support handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an issue report for a support ticket. Separate customer impact, observed behavior, tests performed, findings, and recommended owner.

prompt_templates.title: Engineering Escalation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for moving unresolved work to engineering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an engineering escalation report. Include reproduction context, environment details, ruled-out causes, remaining hypotheses, and evidence gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Incident Review Input
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for operational review after troubleshooting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a troubleshooting report for incident review. Focus on timeline, detection signals, mitigation attempts, unresolved risks, and follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: A login issue was investigated. Password reset worked for some users, but SSO still fails for one region.
output_examples.output: The report identifies the login failure, separates password and SSO findings, notes the affected region, and lists SSO configuration review as the next step.

output_examples.input: A deployment caused elevated errors. Rollback reduced errors, but cache warnings remain in logs.
output_examples.output: The report summarizes the deployment impact, rollback result, remaining cache warnings, and recommended follow-up for log review.

output_examples.input: A user could not export a report. Permissions were checked, and the export service returned intermittent timeouts.
output_examples.output: The report captures user impact, completed permission checks, timeout evidence, and escalation details for the export service owner.

best_practices: Provide the full troubleshooting timeline before requesting the report.
Include exact symptoms, tested fixes, and remaining uncertainty.
Review the generated report before sharing it with another team.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for live diagnostics or monitoring.
Do not omit failed attempts, because they help prevent repeated work.
Do not include secrets, tokens, or private customer data in the session notes.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps create a report after a troubleshooting session by delegating to a named report generator agent.

faq.question: Does it run diagnostic commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill file only instructs the assistant to create a troubleshooting report.

faq.question: Can it write a bug report?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help summarize troubleshooting context into an issue report when enough details are provided.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the problem, affected users or systems, steps tried, results, evidence, and open questions.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains no executable code, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection text.
